---
title: "GPP_Event_Analysis"
output: html_document
---
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE
)
```

```{R}
library(arrow)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(ggplot2)
library(slider)
library(tidyr)
```

## Data preparation
Read in data files
```{R}
df_all_stations <- read_parquet("../data/data_fdk_parquet/fdk_forest_15years.parquet")
```

Filter out sites in koeppen climate codes D and C with cold winters.
```{R}
df_site_info <- read_csv("../data/fdk_site_info.csv")
df_climate_info <- df_site_info |>
  rename("site_id" = sitename) |>
  select(site_id, koeppen_code)
df_sites <- df_all_stations |>
  inner_join(df_climate_info) |>
  filter(koeppen_code %in% c(
      "Cfb", "Cfc",
      "Dfa", "Dfb", "Dfc", "Dfd",
      "Dwa", "Dwb", "Dwc", "Dwd"
    ))
```

**Data quality assessment:**

No quality control variable for `GPP_NT_VUT_REF` and `GPP_DT_VUT_REF` are available. But since these two variables are derived from `NEE_VUT_REF` the control variable `NEE_VUT_REF_QC` is used to filter out GPP recordings of insufficient quality. 
```{R}
df_sites <- df_sites |>
  mutate(
    GPP_DT_clean = if_else(NEE_VUT_REF_QC > 0, GPP_DT_VUT_REF, NA_real_),
    GPP_NT_clean = if_else(NEE_VUT_REF_QC > 0, GPP_NT_VUT_REF, NA_real_)
  )
```

**Calculate running trailing mean:**

```{R}
df_sites <- df_sites %>%
  mutate(
    date = as.Date(TIMESTAMP),
    year = year(date),
    DOY = yday(date)
  ) %>%
  group_by(site_id) %>%
  arrange(date, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    GPP_DT_trailing_4d = slider::slide_dbl(
      GPP_DT_clean,
      ~ if (sum(!is.na(.x)) >= 3) mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_,
      .before = 3,
      .complete = TRUE
    ),
    GPP_NT_trailing_4d = slider::slide_dbl(
      GPP_NT_clean,
      ~ if (sum(!is.na(.x)) >= 3) mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE) else NA_real_,
      .before = 3,
      .complete = TRUE
    )
  ) %>%
  ungroup()
```

### Definition of Threshold for SOS
To define the SOS we first calculate the maximum GPP and minimum GPP per site over the entire period of recordings. Then in a next step we define a threshold for the SOS as follows:
$$
Threshold_{SOS} = GPP_{min} + 0.15 \times (GPP_{max} - GPP_{min})
$$

Calculate maximum and minimum GPP recordings:
```{R}
# set negative values to 0, because biologically values below 0 do not make sense and negative values cause issues when calculating SOS threshold
df_sites <- df_sites %>%
  mutate(
    GPP_DT_trailing_4d_pos = if_else(GPP_DT_trailing_4d < 0, 0, GPP_DT_trailing_4d),
    GPP_NT_trailing_4d_pos = if_else(GPP_NT_trailing_4d < 0, 0, GPP_NT_trailing_4d)
  )

gpp_site_amp <- df_sites %>%
  group_by(site_id) %>%
  summarise(
    GPP_DT_site_max = max(GPP_DT_trailing_4d_pos, na.rm = TRUE),
    GPP_DT_site_min = min(GPP_DT_trailing_4d_pos, na.rm = TRUE),
    GPP_NT_site_max = max(GPP_NT_trailing_4d_pos, na.rm = TRUE),
    GPP_NT_site_min = min(GPP_NT_trailing_4d_pos, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

Calculate SOS threshold:
```{R}
gpp_site_amp <- gpp_site_amp %>%
  mutate(
    GPP_DT_SOS_threshold = GPP_DT_site_min + 0.15 * (GPP_DT_site_max - GPP_DT_site_min),
    GPP_NT_SOS_threshold = GPP_NT_site_min + 0.15 * (GPP_NT_site_max - GPP_NT_site_min)
  )

# combine threshold with sites data set
df_sites <- df_sites %>%
  left_join(gpp_site_amp, by = c("site_id"))
```

Identify frost events after SOS:
```{R}
source("../R/GPP_Frost_Events.R")
```

```{R}
gpp_events_DT <- find_frost_gpp(df_sites, partition = "DT")
gpp_events_NT <- find_frost_gpp(df_sites, partition = "NT")

events_DT <- gpp_events_DT$events
events_NT <- gpp_events_NT$events

df_DT_with_events <- gpp_events_DT$df
df_NT_with_events <- gpp_events_NT$df
```

## Analysis of GPP after known events
### Analysis of GPP trajectory after 2017 events in Europe
Align the event data inspired by the example of Event alignment proposed on blog on Event detection with rgeco package. 
Find the explanation here: https://geco-bern.github.io/rgeco/articles/event_detection.html

Manual function to align events:
```{R}
align_frost_events_manual <- function(df, events, before = 7, after = 14) {
  
  df_clean <- df %>%
    select(-any_of(c("event_id", "dday", "partition")))
  
  events %>%
    select(
      event_id,
      site_id,
      event_year = year,
      start_date,
      end_date,
      Tmin_abs,
      summed_negative_temp
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      window_start = start_date - before,
      window_end = start_date + after
    ) %>%
    rowwise() %>%
    mutate(
      TIMESTAMP = list(seq.Date(window_start, window_end, by = "day"))
    ) %>%
    unnest(TIMESTAMP) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(
      idx_event = as.integer(TIMESTAMP - start_date)
    ) %>%
    left_join(
      df_clean,
      by = c("site_id", "TIMESTAMP")
    )
}
```

Align the event for DT and NT partioned GPP:
```{R}
aligned_DT_manual <- align_frost_events_manual(
  df = df_DT_with_events,
  events = events_DT,
  before = 7,
  after = 28
)

aligned_NT_manual <- align_frost_events_manual(
  df = df_NT_with_events,
  events = events_NT,
  before = 7,
  after = 28
)


```


Calculate DT/NT-GPP anomaly:
```{R}
aligned_DT_manual <- aligned_DT_manual %>%
  group_by(event_id) %>%
  mutate(
    GPP_DT_baseline = mean(
      GPP_DT_trailing_4d_pos[idx_event >= -7 & idx_event <= -1],
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),
    GPP_DT_anomaly_abs = GPP_DT_trailing_4d_pos - GPP_DT_baseline,
    GPP_DT_anomaly_rel = (GPP_DT_trailing_4d_pos - GPP_DT_baseline) / GPP_DT_baseline
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

aligned_NT_manual <- aligned_NT_manual %>%
  group_by(event_id) %>%
  mutate(
    GPP_NT_baseline = mean(
      GPP_NT_trailing_4d_pos[idx_event >= -7 & idx_event <= -1],
      na.rm = TRUE
    ),
    GPP_NT_anomaly_abs = GPP_NT_trailing_4d_pos - GPP_NT_baseline,
    GPP_NT_anomaly_rel = (GPP_NT_trailing_4d_pos - GPP_NT_baseline) / GPP_NT_baseline
  ) %>%
  ungroup()
```

Plot DT-/NT-GPP anomaly:
```{R}
plot_DT_2017_europe <- aligned_DT_manual %>%
  filter(
    event_year == 2017,
    !grepl("^(US|CA)", site_id)
  )

# plot absolute anomaly
ggplot(plot_DT_2017_europe,
       aes(x = idx_event, y = GPP_DT_anomaly_abs, group = event_id)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.35) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = 1),
    fun = mean,
    geom = "line",
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  facet_wrap(~ site_id) +
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to frost event start",
    y = "Absolute GPP anomaly",
    title = "Absolute DT GPP anomaly around frost events in Europe, 2017"
  ) +
  theme_classic()

# plot relative anomaly
ggplot(plot_DT_2017_europe,
       aes(x = idx_event, y = GPP_DT_anomaly_rel, group = event_id)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.35) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = 1),
    fun = mean,
    geom = "line",
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  facet_wrap(~ site_id) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-1, 3)) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(-1, 3, by = 0.2)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to frost event start",
    y = "Relative GPP anomaly",
    title = "Relative DT GPP anomaly around frost events in Europe, 2017"
  ) +
  theme_classic()

plot_NT_2017_europe <- aligned_NT_manual %>%
  filter(
    event_year == 2017,
    !grepl("^(US|CA)", site_id)
  )

# plot absolute anomaly
ggplot(plot_NT_2017_europe,
       aes(x = idx_event, y = GPP_NT_anomaly_abs, group = event_id)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.35) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = 1),
    fun = mean,
    geom = "line",
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  facet_wrap(~ site_id) +
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to frost event start",
    y = "Absolute GPP anomaly",
    title = "Absolute NT GPP anomaly around frost events in Europe, 2017"
  ) +
  theme_classic()

# plot relative anomaly
ggplot(plot_NT_2017_europe,
       aes(x = idx_event, y = GPP_NT_anomaly_rel, group = event_id)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.35) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = 1),
    fun = mean,
    geom = "line",
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  facet_wrap(~ site_id) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-1, 3)) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(-1, 3, by = 0.2)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to frost event start",
    y = "Relative GPP anomaly",
    title = "Relative NT GPP anomaly around frost events in Europe, 2017"
  ) +
  theme_classic()
```

Because GPP is not directly measured but derived from NEE by flux partitioning, event classification can differ between the nighttime and daytime partitioning products. Therefore, DT- and NT-based events were analysed separately and interpreted as a sensitivity of the GPP-based frost-event definition

Plot running mean DT GPP:
```{R}
plot_DT_2017_europe <- aligned_DT_manual %>%
  filter(
    event_year == 2017,
    !grepl("^(US|CA)", site_id),
    idx_event >= -7,
    idx_event <= 21
  )

ggplot(plot_DT_2017_europe,
       aes(x = idx_event,
           y = GPP_DT_trailing_4d_pos,
           group = event_id)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.3) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = 1),
    fun = mean,
    geom = "line",
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  facet_wrap(~ site_id) +
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to frost event start",
    y = "4-day trailing mean GPP",
    title = "DT GPP trajectory around frost events in Europe, 2017",
    subtitle = "Thin lines = individual events, thick line = mean response"
  ) +
  theme_classic()

plot_NT_2017_europe <- aligned_NT_manual %>%
  filter(
    event_year == 2017,
    !grepl("^(US|CA)", site_id),
    idx_event >= -7,
    idx_event <= 21
  )

ggplot(plot_NT_2017_europe,
       aes(x = idx_event,
           y = GPP_DT_trailing_4d_pos,
           group = event_id)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.3) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = 1),
    fun = mean,
    geom = "line",
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  facet_wrap(~ site_id) +
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to frost event start",
    y = "4-day trailing mean GPP",
    title = "NT GPP trajectory around frost events in Europe, 2017",
    subtitle = "Thin lines = individual events, thick line = mean response"
  ) +
  theme_classic()
```

### Analysis of GPP trajectory after 2012 events in north America
Plot DT-/NT-GPP anomaly north america:
```{R}
plot_DT_2012_america <- aligned_DT_manual %>%
  filter(
    event_year == 2012,
    grepl("^(US|CA)", site_id)
  )

# plot absolute anomaly
ggplot(plot_DT_2012_america,
       aes(x = idx_event, y = GPP_DT_anomaly_abs, group = event_id)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.35) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = 1),
    fun = mean,
    geom = "line",
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  facet_wrap(~ site_id) +
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to frost event start",
    y = "Absolute GPP anomaly",
    title = "Absolute DT GPP anomaly around frost events in North America, 2012"
  ) +
  theme_classic()

# plot relative anomaly
ggplot(plot_DT_2012_america,
       aes(x = idx_event, y = GPP_DT_anomaly_rel, group = event_id)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.35) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = 1),
    fun = mean,
    geom = "line",
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  facet_wrap(~ site_id) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-1, 3)) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(-1, 3, by = 0.2)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to frost event start",
    y = "Relative GPP anomaly",
    title = "Relative DT GPP anomaly around frost events in North America, 2012"
  ) +
  theme_classic()

plot_NT_2012_america <- aligned_NT_manual %>%
  filter(
    event_year == 2012,
    grepl("^(US|CA)", site_id)
  )

# plot absolute anomaly
ggplot(plot_NT_2012_america,
       aes(x = idx_event, y = GPP_NT_anomaly_abs, group = event_id)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.35) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = 1),
    fun = mean,
    geom = "line",
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  facet_wrap(~ site_id) +
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to frost event start",
    y = "Absolute GPP anomaly",
    title = "Absolute NT GPP anomaly around frost events in North America, 2012"
  ) +
  theme_classic()

# plot relative anomaly
ggplot(plot_NT_2012_america,
       aes(x = idx_event, y = GPP_NT_anomaly_rel, group = event_id)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.35) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = 1),
    fun = mean,
    geom = "line",
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  facet_wrap(~ site_id) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-1, 3)) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(-1, 3, by = 0.2)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to frost event start",
    y = "Relative GPP anomaly",
    title = "Relative NT GPP anomaly around frost events in North America, 2012"
  ) +
  theme_classic()
```

Plot running mean DT-/NT-GPP in north america:
```{R}
plot_DT_2012_america <- aligned_DT_manual %>%
  filter(
    event_year == 2012,
    grepl("^(US|CA)", site_id),
    idx_event >= -7,
    idx_event <= 21
  )

ggplot(plot_DT_2012_america,
       aes(x = idx_event,
           y = GPP_DT_trailing_4d_pos,
           group = event_id)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.3) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = 1),
    fun = mean,
    geom = "line",
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  facet_wrap(~ site_id) +
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to frost event start",
    y = "4-day trailing mean GPP",
    title = "DT GPP trajectory around frost events in North America, 2012",
    subtitle = "Thin lines = individual events, thick line = mean response"
  ) +
  theme_classic()

plot_NT_2012_america <- aligned_NT_manual %>%
  filter(
    event_year == 2012,
    grepl("^(US|CA)", site_id),
    idx_event >= -7,
    idx_event <= 21
  )

ggplot(plot_NT_2012_america,
       aes(x = idx_event,
           y = GPP_DT_trailing_4d_pos,
           group = event_id)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.3) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = 1),
    fun = mean,
    geom = "line",
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  facet_wrap(~ site_id) +
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to frost event start",
    y = "4-day trailing mean GPP",
    title = "NT GPP trajectory around frost events in North America, 2012",
    subtitle = "Thin lines = individual events, thick line = mean response"
  ) +
  theme_classic()
```

## Analysis of GPP after spring frost across all years per site
Plot relative anomaly of DT GPP after frost:
```{R}
plot_DT_all <- aligned_DT_manual %>%
  mutate(
    region = if_else(grepl("^(US|CA)", site_id), "North America", "Europe")
  )%>%
  filter(
    !is.na(event_id)
  )

# plot relative DT GPP anomaly
ggplot(plot_DT_all,
       aes(x = idx_event, y = GPP_DT_anomaly_rel, group = event_id)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.08) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = 1),
    fun = mean,
    geom = "line",
    linewidth = 1.3
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-1, 3)) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(-1, 3, by = 0.2)
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ region)+
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to frost event start",
    y = "Relative GPP anomaly",
    title = "Pooled relative DT GPP anomaly around all frost events"
  ) +
  theme_classic()

plot_NT_all <- aligned_NT_manual %>%
  mutate(
    region = if_else(grepl("^(US|CA)", site_id), "North America", "Europe")
  )%>%
  filter(
    !is.na(event_id)
  )

# plot relative NT GPP anomaly
ggplot(plot_NT_all,
       aes(x = idx_event, y = GPP_NT_anomaly_rel, group = event_id)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.08) +
  stat_summary(
    aes(group = 1),
    fun = mean,
    geom = "line",
    linewidth = 1.3
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-1, 3)) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(-1, 3, by = 0.2)
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ region)+
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to frost event start",
    y = "Relative GPP anomaly",
    title = "Pooled relative NT GPP anomaly around all frost events"
  ) +
  theme_classic()
```

In the mean the relative anomaly stagnates for DT GPP and reduces slighly the first few days for the relative anomaly of NT GPP. But in the mean there is no trend of a negative relative anomaly. 

### Analyse if the GPP response is related to frost severity
```{R}
# analysis for DT GPP
events_DT <- events_DT %>%
  mutate(
    frost_class = case_when(
      Tmin_abs > -4 ~ "moderate",
      Tmin_abs > -10 ~ "strong",
      TRUE ~ "extreme"
    )
  )

aligned_DT_manual <- aligned_DT_manual %>%
  left_join(
    events_DT %>%
      select(event_id, frost_class),
    by = "event_id"
  )

ggplot(aligned_DT_manual,
       aes(idx_event, GPP_DT_anomaly_rel)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth = 1.2) +
  facet_wrap(~ frost_class)+
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.4, 1.4)) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(-0.4, 1.4, by = 0.2)
  )+
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to frost event start",
    y = "Relative DT GPP anomaly",
    title = "Relative DT GPP anomaly in dependence of frost severity"
  )

# analysis for NT GPP
events_NT <- events_NT %>%
  mutate(
    frost_class = case_when(
      Tmin_abs > -4 ~ "moderate",
      Tmin_abs > -10 ~ "strong",
      TRUE ~ "extreme"
    )
  )

aligned_NT_manual <- aligned_NT_manual %>%
  left_join(
    events_NT %>%
      select(event_id, frost_class),
    by = "event_id"
  )

ggplot(aligned_NT_manual,
       aes(idx_event, GPP_NT_anomaly_rel)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth = 1.2) +
  facet_wrap(~ frost_class) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.4, 1.4)) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(-0.4, 1.4, by = 0.2)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to frost event start",
    y = "Relative NT GPP anomaly",
    title = "Relative NT GPP anomaly in dependence of frost severity"
  )
```

Relative anomaly of DT GPP reaches up to -20% for extreme absolute negative temperatures. For moderate and strong absolute negative temperatures no negative relative DT GPP anomaly is observed. 
Relative anomaly of NT GPP shows strong reduction for extreme and still significant reduction for strong absolute negative temperatures. 

Analyse and Plot the running mean GPP in dependence of frost severity:
```{R}
# running mean DT GPP
ggplot(aligned_DT_manual,
       aes(idx_event, GPP_DT_trailing_4d_pos)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth = 1.2) +
  facet_wrap(~ frost_class) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to frost event start",
    y = "4-day trailing mean DT GPP",
    title = "DT GPP trajectory in dependence of frost severity"
  ) 

# running mean NT GPP
ggplot(aligned_NT_manual,
       aes(idx_event, GPP_NT_trailing_4d_pos)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth = 1.2) +
  facet_wrap(~ frost_class) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to frost event start",
    y = "4-day trailing mean NT GPP",
    title = "NT GPP trajectory in dependence of frost severity"
  )
```

Plotting the trailing mean of DT GPP for all three classes of frost severity a clear reduction is visible for extreme absolute negative temperatures for the first 5 days. For strong absolute temperatures there is also a slight reduction in the trailing mean DT GPP. 

Plotting the trailing mean of NT GPP for all three classes of frost severity a clear reduction is visible for extreme absolute negative temperatures for the first 10 days. For strong absolute temperatures there is also a clear reduction for the first 3-4 days.

Analyze trailing GPP based on summed negative temperature:
```{R}
# categorize and plot DT GPP
q_DT <- quantile(
  aligned_DT_manual$summed_negative_temp,
  probs = c(0.25, 0.50),
  na.rm = TRUE
)

aligned_DT_manual <- aligned_DT_manual %>%
  mutate(
    frost_load_class = case_when(
      summed_negative_temp <= q_DT[1] ~ "high frost load",
      summed_negative_temp <= q_DT[2] ~ "medium frost load",
      TRUE ~ "low frost load"
    )
  )

ggplot(aligned_DT_manual,
       aes(x = idx_event, y = GPP_DT_trailing_4d_pos)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth = 1.2) +
  facet_wrap(~ frost_load_class) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to frost event start",
    y = "4-day trailing mean DT GPP",
    title = "DT GPP trajectory by cumulative frost severity"
  )

# categorize and plot NT GPP
q_NT <- quantile(
  aligned_NT_manual$summed_negative_temp,
  probs = c(0.25, 0.50),
  na.rm = TRUE
)

aligned_NT_manual <- aligned_NT_manual %>%
  mutate(
    frost_load_class = case_when(
      summed_negative_temp <= q_NT[1] ~ "high frost load",
      summed_negative_temp <= q_NT[2] ~ "medium frost load",
      TRUE ~ "low frost load"
    )
  )

ggplot(aligned_NT_manual,
       aes(x = idx_event, y = GPP_NT_trailing_4d_pos)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth = 1.2) +
  facet_wrap(~ frost_load_class) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to frost event start",
    y = "4-day trailing mean NT GPP",
    title = "NT GPP trajectory by cumulative frost severity"
  )
```

It is clearly visible, that the decrease in trailing GPP is most significant for high frost load events, meaning summed negative temperature are in the lower 25% quantile. For medium frost load almost no decrease and for low frost load no decrease at all.


### Analyse the impact of the GPP-Level before the frost event
Categorize events based on pre-event GPP:
```{R}
# calculate and plot for DT GPP
aligned_DT_manual <- aligned_DT_manual %>%
  mutate(
    activity_class = case_when(
      GPP_DT_baseline < 2 ~ "low",
      GPP_DT_baseline < 5 ~ "medium",
      TRUE ~ "high"
    )
  )

ggplot(aligned_DT_manual,
       aes(x = idx_event, y = GPP_DT_trailing_4d_pos)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth = 1.2) +
  facet_wrap(~ activity_class) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to frost event start",
    y = "4-day trailing mean DT GPP",
    title = "DT GPP trajectory in dependence of pre-frost GPP activity"
  )

# calculate and plot for NT GPP
aligned_NT_manual <- aligned_NT_manual %>%
  mutate(
    activity_class = case_when(
      GPP_NT_baseline < 2 ~ "low",
      GPP_NT_baseline < 5 ~ "medium",
      TRUE ~ "high"
    )
  )

ggplot(aligned_NT_manual,
       aes(x = idx_event, y = GPP_NT_trailing_4d_pos)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth = 1.2) +
  facet_wrap(~ activity_class) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to frost event start",
    y = "4-day trailing mean NT GPP",
    title = "NT GPP trajectory in dependence of pre-frost GPP activity"
  )
```

For DT GPP there is a decrease in trailing GPP for high base level GPP. But for some reason the peak is before the event and then the lowest is when the event hits. 
For NT GPP there is a decrease in all cases, but the decrease is most significant for high base level GPP. 

```{R}
# for DT GPP
qs_DT <- quantile(aligned_DT_manual$GPP_DT_baseline,
         probs = c(0.5, 0.75),
         na.rm = TRUE)
q1_DT <- qs_DT[1]
q2_DT <- qs_DT[2]

aligned_DT_manual <- aligned_DT_manual %>%
  mutate(
    activity_class = case_when(
      GPP_DT_baseline < q1_DT ~ "low",
      GPP_DT_baseline < q2_DT ~ "medium",
      TRUE ~ "high"
    )
  )
ggplot(aligned_DT_manual,
       aes(x = idx_event, y = GPP_DT_trailing_4d_pos)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth = 1.2) +
  facet_wrap(~ activity_class) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to frost event start",
    y = "4-day trailing mean DT GPP",
    title = "DT GPP trajectory in dependence of pre-frost GPP activity"
  )

# for NT GPP
qs_NT <- quantile(
  aligned_NT_manual$GPP_NT_baseline,
  probs = c(0.5, 0.75),
  na.rm = TRUE
)

q1_NT <- qs_NT[1]
q2_NT <- qs_NT[2]

aligned_NT_manual <- aligned_NT_manual %>%
  mutate(
    activity_class = case_when(
      GPP_NT_baseline < q1_NT ~ "low",
      GPP_NT_baseline < q2_NT ~ "medium",
      TRUE ~ "high"
    )
  )
ggplot(aligned_NT_manual,
       aes(x = idx_event, y = GPP_DT_trailing_4d_pos)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth = 1.2) +
  facet_wrap(~ activity_class) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to frost event start",
    y = "4-day trailing mean DT GPP",
    title = "NT GPP trajectory in dependence of pre-frost GPP activity"
  )
```

For the 75% quantile the trailing DT and NT GPP reduced due to frost event. 

### Analysis based on vegetation type and based on koeppen climate code
Categorize based on vegetation type:
```{R}
# categorize and plot DT GPP
aligned_DT_manual <- aligned_DT_manual %>%
  mutate(
    forest_type = case_when(
      igbp_land_use == "DBF" ~ "Deciduous broadleaf forest",
      igbp_land_use == "ENF" ~ "Evergreen needleleaf forest",
      igbp_land_use == "MF"  ~ "Mixed forest",
      TRUE ~ "Other forest"
    )
  )

ggplot(aligned_DT_manual,
       aes(x = idx_event, y = GPP_DT_trailing_4d_pos)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth = 1.2) +
  facet_wrap(~ forest_type) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to frost event start",
    y = "4-day trailing mean DT GPP",
    title = "DT GPP trajectory after frost events by forest type"
  )

# categorize and plot NT GPP
aligned_NT_manual <- aligned_NT_manual %>%
  mutate(
    forest_type = case_when(
      igbp_land_use == "DBF" ~ "Deciduous broadleaf forest",
      igbp_land_use == "ENF" ~ "Evergreen needleleaf forest",
      igbp_land_use == "MF"  ~ "Mixed forest",
      TRUE ~ "Other forest"
    )
  )

ggplot(aligned_NT_manual,
       aes(x = idx_event, y = GPP_NT_trailing_4d_pos)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth = 1.2) +
  facet_wrap(~ forest_type) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to frost event start",
    y = "4-day trailing mean NT GPP",
    title = "NT GPP trajectory after frost events by forest type"
  )
```

For trailing mean DT GPP we see a reduction after frost for mixed forests but only a very slight decrease/stagnation for evergreen needleleafe forests.
For trailing mean NT GPP we see a very strong and lasting decrease for Decidous broadleaf forests. For mixed forests a short-term decrease can also be observed. Evergreen needleleaf forest only very slightly. 

Categorize based on koeppen climate code:
```{R}
# categorize and plot DT GPP
aligned_DT_manual <- aligned_DT_manual %>%
  mutate(
    koeppen_class = case_when(
      grepl("^C", koeppen_code) ~ "Temperate",
      grepl("^D", koeppen_code) ~ "Continental",
      grepl("^E", koeppen_code) ~ "Polar",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    )
  )

ggplot(aligned_DT_manual,
       aes(x = idx_event, y = GPP_DT_trailing_4d_pos)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth = 1.2) +
  facet_wrap(~ koeppen_class) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to frost event start",
    y = "4-day trailing mean DT GPP",
    title = "DT GPP trajectory after frost events by Köppen climate class"
  )

# categorize and plot NT GPP
aligned_NT_manual <- aligned_NT_manual %>%
  mutate(
    koeppen_class = case_when(
      grepl("^C", koeppen_code) ~ "Temperate",
      grepl("^D", koeppen_code) ~ "Continental",
      grepl("^E", koeppen_code) ~ "Polar",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    )
  )

ggplot(aligned_NT_manual,
       aes(x = idx_event, y = GPP_NT_trailing_4d_pos)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth = 1.2) +
  facet_wrap(~ koeppen_class) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to frost event start",
    y = "4-day trailing mean NT GPP",
    title = "NT GPP trajectory after frost events by Köppen climate class"
  )
```

When comparing continental vs. Temperate climates, there does not appear to be a difference in the response to frost events. For both cases there is a short-term reduction of trailing GPP followed by an increase. 

Plot the relative anomaly of trailing mean GPP:
```{R}
# DT: relative GPP anomaly by Köppen climate class
ggplot(aligned_DT_manual,
       aes(x = idx_event, y = GPP_DT_anomaly_rel)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth = 1.2) +
  facet_wrap(~ koeppen_class) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-1.0, 2.0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(-1.0, 2.0, by = 0.25)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to frost event start",
    y = "Relative DT GPP anomaly",
    title = "Relative DT GPP anomaly after frost events by Köppen climate class"
  )

# NT: relative GPP anomaly by Köppen climate class
ggplot(aligned_NT_manual,
       aes(x = idx_event, y = GPP_NT_anomaly_rel)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", linewidth = 1.2) +
  facet_wrap(~ koeppen_class) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-1.0, 2.0)) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = seq(-1.0, 2.0, by = 0.25)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to frost event start",
    y = "Relative NT GPP anomaly",
    title = "Relative NT GPP anomaly after frost events by Köppen climate class"
  )
```


### Analysis of mean seasonal cycle
Plot mean seasonal cycle and seasonal cycle of event year
```{R}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# SETTINGS
# ------------------------------------------------------------

# Choose GPP variable
gpp_var <- "GPP_DT_trailing_4d_pos"   # or "GPP_NT_trailing_4d_pos"

# Choose corresponding frost event table
events_use <- events_DT              # or events_NT

# Define significant frost events
significant_events <- events_use %>%
  filter(Tmin_abs <= -4) %>%          # adjust threshold if needed
  distinct(site_id, event_year = year)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Calculate SOS for each site-year
# ------------------------------------------------------------

sos_site_year <- df_sites %>%
  group_by(site_id, year) %>%
  arrange(date, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  summarise(
    SOS_DOY = DOY[
      which(.data[[gpp_var]] >= first(GPP_DT_SOS_threshold))[1]
    ],
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(SOS_DOY))

# Use NT threshold if NT-GPP is selected
if (gpp_var == "GPP_NT_trailing_4d_pos") {
  sos_site_year <- df_sites %>%
    group_by(site_id, year) %>%
    arrange(date, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
    summarise(
      SOS_DOY = DOY[
        which(.data[[gpp_var]] >= first(GPP_NT_SOS_threshold))[1]
      ],
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(SOS_DOY))
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Prepare GPP data
# ------------------------------------------------------------

gpp_seasonal_df <- df_sites %>%
  select(site_id, date, year, DOY, all_of(gpp_var)) %>%
  rename(GPP = all_of(gpp_var)) %>%
  left_join(sos_site_year, by = c("site_id", "year")) %>%
  mutate(
    day_rel_SOS = DOY - SOS_DOY
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(GPP))

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Calculate mean seasonal cycle across all station-years
#    Black line: all available station-years
# ------------------------------------------------------------

mean_cycle_doy <- gpp_seasonal_df %>%
  group_by(DOY) %>%
  summarise(
    GPP_mean = mean(GPP, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

mean_cycle_sos <- gpp_seasonal_df %>%
  filter(day_rel_SOS >= -150,
         day_rel_SOS <= 365) %>%
  group_by(day_rel_SOS) %>%
  summarise(
    GPP_mean = mean(GPP, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = sum(!is.na(GPP)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(n>=10)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4) Calculate mean seasonal cycle for frost-event years
#    Red line: only station-years with significant frost after SOS
# ------------------------------------------------------------

eventyear_df <- gpp_seasonal_df %>%
  inner_join(significant_events, by = c("site_id", "year" = "event_year"))

event_cycle_doy <- eventyear_df %>%
  group_by(DOY) %>%
  summarise(
    GPP_event = mean(GPP, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

event_cycle_sos <- eventyear_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(day_rel_SOS)) %>%
  group_by(day_rel_SOS) %>%
  summarise(
    GPP_event = mean(GPP, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 5) Plot A: Seasonal cycle by day of year
# ------------------------------------------------------------

ggplot() +
  geom_line(
    data = mean_cycle_doy,
    aes(x = DOY, y = GPP_mean),
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = event_cycle_doy,
    aes(x = DOY, y = GPP_event),
    color = "red",
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Day of year",
    y = "GPP",
    title = "Mean seasonal cycle of GPP",
    subtitle = "Black = all station-years, red = station-years with significant frost after SOS"
  ) +
  theme_classic()

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 6) Plot B: Seasonal cycle relative to SOS
# ------------------------------------------------------------

ggplot() +
  geom_line(
    data = mean_cycle_sos,
    aes(x = day_rel_SOS, y = GPP_mean),
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = event_cycle_sos,
    aes(x = day_rel_SOS, y = GPP_event),
    color = "red",
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    x = "Days relative to SOS",
    y = "GPP",
    title = "Mean seasonal cycle of GPP relative to SOS",
    subtitle = "Black = all station-years, red = station-years with significant frost after SOS"
  ) +
  theme_classic()
```

Averaged over all stations it is visible that the seasonal cycle in years with significant frost after SOS is rising faster in early spring but the yearly peak is lower then on average over all station years. In addition GPP seems to be slightly higher in the last quarter of the year for years with significant frost after SOS. 

When comparing the seasonal cycle relative to the SOS the trajectory of all station years and the years with significant frost after SOS is very similar until the SOS. Shortly after the SOS the years with frost after SOS show a visible reduction in GPP followed by a rise again. The peak is later after the SOS and also lower then the average over all station years. As a consequence the reduction is also shifted. 

Plot mean seasonal cycle and seasonal cycle of event years 2012/2017 per station:
```{R}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Define reference event year by region
# ------------------------------------------------------------

gpp_seasonal_df_region <- gpp_seasonal_df %>%
  mutate(
    country_code = substr(site_id, 1, 2),
    target_year = if_else(country_code %in% c("US", "CA"), 2012, 2017)
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Mean seasonal cycle per site across all years
# ------------------------------------------------------------

mean_cycle_doy_site <- gpp_seasonal_df_region %>%
  group_by(site_id, DOY) %>%
  summarise(
    GPP_mean = mean(GPP, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

mean_cycle_sos_site <- gpp_seasonal_df_region %>%
  filter(day_rel_SOS >= -120,
         day_rel_SOS <= 250) %>%
  group_by(site_id, day_rel_SOS) %>%
  summarise(
    GPP_mean = mean(GPP, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3) Seasonal cycle of target event year per site
#    Europe: 2017
#    North America: 2012
# ------------------------------------------------------------

targetyear_df <- gpp_seasonal_df_region %>%
  filter(year == target_year)

target_cycle_doy_site <- targetyear_df %>%
  group_by(site_id, DOY, year) %>%
  summarise(
    GPP_target = mean(GPP, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

target_cycle_sos_site <- targetyear_df %>%
  filter(day_rel_SOS >= -120,
         day_rel_SOS <= 250) %>%
  group_by(site_id, day_rel_SOS, year) %>%
  summarise(
    GPP_target = mean(GPP, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4) Save one DOY plot per site as PNG
# ------------------------------------------------------------

dir.create("../figures/seasonal_cycle_plots_doy", showWarnings = FALSE)

sites_to_plot <- unique(mean_cycle_doy_site$site_id)

for (s in sites_to_plot) {
  
  target_year_s <- gpp_seasonal_df_region %>%
    filter(site_id == s) %>%
    distinct(target_year) %>%
    pull(target_year)
  
  p_doy <- ggplot() +
    geom_line(
      data = mean_cycle_doy_site %>% filter(site_id == s),
      aes(x = DOY, y = GPP_mean),
      color = "black",
      linewidth = 1
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = target_cycle_doy_site %>% filter(site_id == s),
      aes(x = DOY, y = GPP_target),
      color = "red",
      linewidth = 1
    ) +
    labs(
      x = "Day of year",
      y = "GPP",
      title = paste0(s, " - GPP seasonal cycle"),
      subtitle = paste0(
        "Black = mean over all years, red = ",
        target_year_s
      )
    ) +
    theme_classic()
  
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("../figures/seasonal_cycle_plots_doy/", s, "_GPP_seasonal_cycle_DOY.png"),
    plot = p_doy,
    width = 7,
    height = 5,
    dpi = 300
  )
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 5) Save one SOS-relative plot per site as PNG
# ------------------------------------------------------------

dir.create("../figures/seasonal_cycle_plots_sos", showWarnings = FALSE)

sites_to_plot <- unique(mean_cycle_sos_site$site_id)

for (s in sites_to_plot) {
  
  target_year_s <- gpp_seasonal_df_region %>%
    filter(site_id == s) %>%
    distinct(target_year) %>%
    pull(target_year)
  
  p_sos <- ggplot() +
    geom_line(
      data = mean_cycle_sos_site %>% filter(site_id == s),
      aes(x = day_rel_SOS, y = GPP_mean),
      color = "black",
      linewidth = 1
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = target_cycle_sos_site %>% filter(site_id == s),
      aes(x = day_rel_SOS, y = GPP_target),
      color = "red",
      linewidth = 1
    ) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
    labs(
      x = "Days relative to SOS",
      y = "GPP",
      title = paste0(s, " - GPP seasonal cycle relative to SOS"),
      subtitle = paste0(
        "Black = mean over all years, red = ",
        target_year_s
      )
    ) +
    theme_classic()
  
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0("../figures/seasonal_cycle_plots_sos/", s, "_GPP_seasonal_cycle_SOS.png"),
    plot = p_sos,
    width = 7,
    height = 5,
    dpi = 300
  )
}

```

Plot nonfrost vs. frost year per site
```{R}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1) Calculate mean seasonal cycle per site across all years
#    Black line: all available years per site
# ------------------------------------------------------------

mean_cycle_doy_site <- gpp_seasonal_df %>%
  group_by(site_id, DOY) %>%
  summarise(
    GPP_mean = mean(GPP, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

mean_cycle_sos_site <- gpp_seasonal_df %>%
  filter(day_rel_SOS >= -150,
         day_rel_SOS <= 365) %>%
  group_by(site_id, day_rel_SOS) %>%
  summarise(
    GPP_mean = mean(GPP, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = sum(!is.na(GPP)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(n >= 10)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2) Calculate mean seasonal cycle for frost-event years per site
#    Red line: only years with significant frost after SOS per site
# ------------------------------------------------------------

eventyear_df <- gpp_seasonal_df %>%
  inner_join(significant_events, by = c("site_id", "year" = "event_year"))

event_cycle_doy_site <- eventyear_df %>%
  group_by(site_id, DOY) %>%
  summarise(
    GPP_event = mean(GPP, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

event_cycle_sos_site <- eventyear_df %>%
  filter(day_rel_SOS >= -150,
         day_rel_SOS <= 365) %>%
  group_by(site_id, day_rel_SOS) %>%
  summarise(
    GPP_event = mean(GPP, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = sum(!is.na(GPP)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(n >= 1)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Save DOY plot per site
# ------------------------------------------------------------

dir.create("../figures/seasonal_cycle_DOY_by_site", showWarnings = FALSE)

for (s in unique(mean_cycle_doy_site$site_id)) {
  
  p_doy <- ggplot() +
    geom_line(
      data = mean_cycle_doy_site %>% filter(site_id == s),
      aes(x = DOY, y = GPP_mean),
      color = "black",
      linewidth = 1
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = event_cycle_doy_site %>% filter(site_id == s),
      aes(x = DOY, y = GPP_event),
      color = "red",
      linewidth = 1
    ) +
    labs(
      x = "Day of year",
      y = "GPP",
      title = paste0(s, " - Mean seasonal cycle of GPP"),
      subtitle = "Black = all years, red = years with significant frost after SOS"
    ) +
    theme_classic()
  
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0(
      "../figures/seasonal_cycle_DOY_by_site/",
      s,
      "_GPP_mean_cycle_event_years_DOY.png"
    ),
    plot = p_doy,
    width = 7,
    height = 5,
    dpi = 300
  )
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Save SOS-relative plot per site
# ------------------------------------------------------------

dir.create("../figures/seasonal_cycle_SOS_by_site", showWarnings = FALSE)

for (s in unique(mean_cycle_sos_site$site_id)) {
  
  p_sos <- ggplot() +
    geom_line(
      data = mean_cycle_sos_site %>% filter(site_id == s),
      aes(x = day_rel_SOS, y = GPP_mean),
      color = "black",
      linewidth = 1
    ) +
    geom_line(
      data = event_cycle_sos_site %>% filter(site_id == s),
      aes(x = day_rel_SOS, y = GPP_event),
      color = "red",
      linewidth = 1
    ) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
    labs(
      x = "Days relative to SOS",
      y = "GPP",
      title = paste0(s, " - Mean seasonal cycle of GPP relative to SOS"),
      subtitle = "Black = all years, red = years with significant frost after SOS"
    ) +
    theme_classic()
  
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0(
      "../figures/seasonal_cycle_SOS_by_site/",
      s,
      "_GPP_mean_cycle_event_years_SOS.png"
    ),
    plot = p_sos,
    width = 7,
    height = 5,
    dpi = 300
  )
}

```

When looking at the plots relative to the SOS per site for the european sites, it is almost always the case, that the peak is later relative to the SOS then on average across all site years. In addition almost every site shows a significant drop in GPP close after the SOS due to the significant frost event. However, for many of the sites the peak of the accumulated GPP is higher for the years with a significant frost event, then on average over all site years. 

When looking at the plots relative to the SOS per site for the american sites, it is almost always the case, that there is a visible drop shortly after the SOS or sometimes even multiple times for the years with a significant frost after SOS. Therefore the curve is below the curve of the average across years. In some cases the peak is significantly higher than on average and also a lot later relative to the SOS then on average. The significantly larger peaks could be a sign of overcompensation. 


Plot relative difference of frost years vs. all years
```{R}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Relative difference (%) of frost years vs all years
# ------------------------------------------------------------

relative_diff_sos_site <- event_cycle_sos_site %>%
  left_join(
    mean_cycle_sos_site,
    by = c("site_id", "day_rel_SOS")
  ) %>%
  filter(
    day_rel_SOS >= -150,
    day_rel_SOS <= 365,
    !is.na(GPP_event),
    !is.na(GPP_mean),
    GPP_mean > 0.1
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    rel_diff_percent = 100 * (GPP_event - GPP_mean) / GPP_mean
  )

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Save relative difference plot per site
# ------------------------------------------------------------

dir.create("../figures/relative_difference_SOS_by_site", showWarnings = FALSE)

for (s in unique(relative_diff_sos_site$site_id)) {
  
  p <- ggplot(relative_diff_sos_site %>% filter(site_id == s),
              aes(x = day_rel_SOS, y = rel_diff_percent)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_line(color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
    coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-150, 365),
                    ylim = c(-100, 100)) +
    labs(
      x = "Days relative to SOS",
      y = "Relative GPP difference (%)",
      title = paste0(s, " - Relative GPP difference"),
      subtitle = "100 × (GPP_frost - GPP_all) / GPP_all"
    ) +
    theme_classic()
  
  ggsave(
    filename = paste0(
      "../figures/relative_difference_SOS_by_site/",
      s,
      "_relative_GPP_difference_SOS.png"
    ),
    plot = p,
    width = 7,
    height = 5,
    dpi = 300
  )
}
```



Plot timing of peak GPP per site year
```{R}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Peak GPP timing per site-year
# ------------------------------------------------------------

peak_gpp_site_year <- gpp_cycle_df %>%
  group_by(site_id, year, frost_class) %>%
  summarise(
    peak_GPP = max(GPP, na.rm = TRUE),
    DOY_peak = DOY[which.max(GPP)],
    day_rel_SOS_peak = day_rel_SOS[which.max(GPP)],
    SOS_DOY = first(SOS_DOY),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(is.finite(peak_GPP),
         !is.na(DOY_peak),
         !is.na(day_rel_SOS_peak))

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Save peak timing plots
# ------------------------------------------------------------

dir.create("../figures/GPP_peak_timing", showWarnings = FALSE)

p_peak_sos <- ggplot(peak_gpp_site_year,
                     aes(x = frost_class, y = day_rel_SOS_peak, fill = frost_class)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, size = 1.8, alpha = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ site_id, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Non-frost years" = "grey70",
               "Frost years" = "red")
  ) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Timing of peak GPP relative to SOS",
    title = "Timing of peak GPP in frost vs non-frost years"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)
  )

ggsave(
  filename = "../figures/GPP_peak_timing/peak_GPP_timing_relative_to_SOS.png",
  plot = p_peak_sos,
  width = 12,
  height = 8,
  dpi = 300
)

p_peak_doy <- ggplot(peak_gpp_site_year,
                     aes(x = frost_class, y = DOY_peak, fill = frost_class)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, size = 1.8, alpha = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ site_id, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Non-frost years" = "grey70",
               "Frost years" = "red")
  ) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "DOY of peak GPP",
    title = "Calendar timing of peak GPP in frost vs non-frost years"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)
  )

ggsave(
  filename = "../figures/GPP_peak_timing/peak_GPP_timing_DOY.png",
  plot = p_peak_doy,
  width = 12,
  height = 8,
  dpi = 300
)
```

When looking at the timing of peak GPP relative to SOS, then for most sites the median is higher for years with frost, compared to years without frost.
